package service_test

import (
	"testing"

	"github.com/stretchr/testify/assert"
)

// Positive scenario for table_driven_tests_mix_multiple_domains_in_one_cases_slice.
// Rule intent: Oversized case tables that mix unrelated behaviors into one monolithic test.

type User struct {
	Name  string
	Email string
}

type Order struct {
	Product string
	Amount  float64
}

type Payment struct {
	OrderID string
	Method  string
	Status  string
}

// Anti-pattern: a single table-driven test mixing User, Order, and Payment domains
// in one cases slice — these are unrelated behaviors that should be tested separately.
func TestMixedDomains(t *testing.T) {
	cases := []struct {
		name    string
		user    User
		order   Order
		payment Payment
	}{
		{
			name: "user creates account",
			user: User{Name: "Alice", Email: "alice@example.com"},
		},
		{
			name:  "order is placed",
			order: Order{Product: "Widget", Amount: 29.99},
		},
		{
			name: "user updates profile",
			user: User{Name: "Alice Updated", Email: "alice.new@example.com"},
		},
		{
			name:    "payment is processed",
			payment: Payment{OrderID: "ord-1", Method: "credit_card", Status: "success"},
		},
		{
			name:  "order is cancelled",
			order: Order{Product: "Gadget", Amount: 49.99},
		},
		{
			name:    "payment is refunded",
			payment: Payment{OrderID: "ord-2", Method: "paypal", Status: "refunded"},
		},
		{
			name: "user is deleted",
			user: User{Name: "Bob", Email: "bob@example.com"},
		},
	}

	for _, tc := range cases {
		t.Run(tc.name, func(t *testing.T) {
			// Anti-pattern: branching logic to handle multiple unrelated domains
			if tc.user.Name != "" {
				assert.NotEmpty(t, tc.user.Email)
			}
			if tc.order.Product != "" {
				assert.Greater(t, tc.order.Amount, 0.0)
			}
			if tc.payment.OrderID != "" {
				assert.Contains(t, []string{"success", "refunded", "pending"}, tc.payment.Status)
			}
		})
	}
}
